title: CP vs AP — The Economic Calculus

variables.name: Write rate
variables.source: Peak load from 100M orders/day ÷ 8 peak hours

cp.monthlyPenalty: $2,500 — additional DB capacity for synchronous replication

calculation.breakeven: At 1,200 TPS, CP becomes cheaper because reconciliation costs grow linearly with volume (more conflicts = more reconciliation overhead) while DB capacity costs grow sub-linearly.

cp.monthlyPenalty: $1,200 — Write-Through: doubles write IO, additional Redis cluster

ap.monthlyPenalty: $400 — Write-Behind: single Redis cluster, risk of data loss

calculation.breakeven: Write-Through costs 3x more than Write-Behind at 200 TPS. At 1,000 TPS, Write-Through must be used because Write-Behind's data loss window becomes unacceptable.

title: Monolith vs Microservices — Total Cost of Operations

cp.monthlyPenalty: $15,000 — Monolith: simpler infra ($3K), but higher coordination cost ($12K in meetings + release mgmt)

ap.monthlyPenalty: $22,000 — Microservices: higher infra ($10K for service mesh + monitoring), lower coordination ($3K), higher oncall ($9K for 5 services)

calculation.breakeven: At 8+ services, microservices TCO becomes cheaper because monolith coordination costs grow as O(n²) while microservices coordination grows as O(n).

title: Strong vs Eventual Consistency — Cost Analysis

cp.monthlyPenalty: $12,000 — Spanner-like setup: 3 regions, 7 nodes each, synchronous replication

ap.monthlyPenalty: $4,500 — DynamoDB-like: 3 regions, global tables, CRDT conflict resolution

calculation.breakeven: At write rates below 500 TPS, strong consistency costs 2.6x more. At 2,000+ TPS with 3+ regions, CRDT conflict resolution overhead explodes (O(n²) conflict pairs) and strong consistency with partitioned leader becomes cheaper.

title: RAP Managed vs Unmanaged — Development Cost

variables.name: Entity complexity
variables.source: Child entities with standard CRUD + 2 custom actions

cp.monthlyPenalty: $52,500 — Managed: 15 entities × 3 days/entity × $1,200/day = $54K. Draft + locking included.

ap.monthlyPenalty: $180,000 — Unmanaged: 15 entities × 10 days/entity × $1,200/day = $180K. Draft + locking are manual.

calculation.breakeven: For any entity with >40% standard CRUD, managed is 3.3x cheaper in development cost. Unmanaged is only cost-effective when >60% of operations are custom (e.g., legacy BAPI wrappers).

ap.monthlyPenalty: $8,000 — Single HANA instance: scale-up to 6TB. But single point of failure and scaling limit at 20 TB.

calculation.breakeven: At 20 TB, sharding becomes mandatory regardless of cost — single-node can't hold the data. Sharding also improves blast radius: 1 corrupt shard affects 10% of tenants, not 100%.
